title: Design Patterns — Creational
learningObjectives: Defend **Creational design patterns** choices with trade-offs, failure domains, and metrics
Contrast patterns and anti-patterns interviewers probe with follow-up questions
Sketch boundaries, data flows, and consistency models on a whiteboard
Relate scalability, cost, and operational complexity to team constraints
Use structured responses: requirements, options, decision, verification
Prepare STAR-style stories where architecture decisions had measurable impact

sections.title: Why Design Patterns — Creational matters
sections.content: **Creational** patterns organize **object** **birth**. Senior answers compare **testability**, **thread** **safety**, and **Spring** **alternatives** to textbook **Singleton**.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Design Patterns — Creational
sections.content:
### Day 78 — **Creational** **patterns**

### 1. Singleton
**Global** **state** **risk** vs **true** **single** **coordination**.

### 2. Factory family
Hide **construction** **details** behind **interfaces**.

### 3. Builder
**Stepwise** **construction** with **validation**.

### 4. Prototype
**Clone** when **subclasses** proliferate.

### 5. Modern Java
**Records**, **DI**, **modules** change defaults.

### 6. Story
**DCL** **Singleton** **bug** under **reorder**—**enum** **wins** **interview** **credibility**.

sections.title: Basic — Pattern names
Intermediate — Builder intent
sections.output: builder: optional fields + validation before build()

sections.title: Advanced — Enum singleton note
sections.output: prefer enum singleton over double-checked locking

sections.title: Factory produces products
Exercise — Design Patterns — Creational (Day 78 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** one line: enum: serialization/thread safety; DCL: easy to get wrong

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: **Singleton** when **justified**?
conceptual.answer: Truly single coordinated resource—often better as **DI**-scoped bean or explicit platform service. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Creational design patterns**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Truly single coordinated resource—often better as **DI**-scoped bean or explicit platform service. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Truly single coordinated resource—often better as **DI**-scoped bean or explicit platform Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

conceptual.question: **Factory** **Method** vs **Abstract** **Factory**?
conceptual.answer: One product family method vs families of related products—interviews want boundary clarity. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Design Patterns — Creational** deploy correlates with **5xx** spike.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze risky changes; open **SLO** dashboard.

**(2) Root cause** **Config** regression or **dependency** **saturation**.

**(3) Fix** Rollback or **toggle** **flag**; restore **SLO** then root-cause.

**(4) Prevention** Add **canary** gate and **diff** **alerts** on **golden** metrics. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.answer: Share **SLO** **burn**, **customer** **regions** affected, **mitigation** steps in flight, and **explicit** unknowns. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Pattern | Recall |
|---|---|
| Singleton | One instance |
| Factory Method | Subclass decides |
| Abstract Factory | Product families |
| Builder | Stepwise build |
| Prototype | Clone instance |
| Enum singleton | Safe Java idiom |
| DCL | Memory model risk |
| DI | Framework singleton |
| Registry | Named creators |
| Anti | Global state abuse |
